top_categories.workout_context: UCI Cyprus Gran Fondo's mountain passes forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 63 miles.
63 miles on mountain passes burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
Race-pace efforts that mimic UCI Cyprus Gran Fondo's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before April.
104ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
6,562ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
At UCI Cyprus Gran Fondo, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Base fitness for UCI Cyprus Gran Fondo. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At UCI Cyprus Gran Fondo, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of UCI Cyprus Gran Fondo. Train it until it feels boring.
6,562ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on mountain passes. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Smooth pedaling at 63 miles saves muscle. Bad technique at mile 10 is invisible. At mile 80 it's a cramp.

race_overlay.heat: April in Paphos, Cyprus — Mediterranean spring: sunny, cool breezes, ideal cycling conditions. Begin heat acclimatization 10–14 days before UCI Cyprus Gran Fondo — 20–30min sauna sessions or midday rides in full kit. Pre-load sodium 48 hours out. Target 500–750ml/hr with electrolytes on race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for UCI Cyprus Gran Fondo’s 63 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at UCI Cyprus Gran Fondo. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Highly technical terrain at UCI Cyprus Gran Fondo demands practice on similar surfaces. Ride mountain passes at race-day cadence weekly. Practice cornering, descending, and power delivery on unstable surfaces. Dial in tire pressure before race week — 5 PSI wrong costs you 15+ minutes over 63 miles.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Gravel Specific, Durability, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 63 miles of mountain passes in Paphos, Cyprus.
